> [!NOTE]> This is a **FORMAT document** GÇö a plain-text Markdown file that carries its own schema in the YAML frontmatter. New to FORMAT? The [onboarding guide](https://format.innv0.com/docs/onboarding) walks you through what this is and how to open it in the visual editor.# <!-- block: concepts --> procedureThis procedure defines the workflow for creating, reviewing, approving, and publishing Markdown documentation within an organization using Git and GitHub. The process ensures knowledge is captured consistently, reviewed for quality and accuracy, and published to a central knowledge base. Every document goes through a structured lifecycle: drafting, peer review, technical review, approval, and publication. Git provides version control, branching, and full traceability; GitHub enables collaborative reviews via pull requests. The result is a living knowledge base that evolves with the organization while maintaining quality standards.# <!-- block: concepts --> work1. <!-- block: work --> Draft Document  ```yaml  step_type: "task"  next: "Submit Pull Request"  tool: "Markdown Editor"  ```The author creates or updates a Markdown document following the organization's style guide. Content is written in a feature branch off the main repository. The author saves progress with regular commits and writes a meaningful commit message for each logical change.2. <!-- block: work --> Submit Pull Request  ```yaml  step_type: "task"  next: "Peer Review"  output: "Pull Request"  output_status: "draft"  tool: "GitHub"  ```Once the draft is complete, the author pushes the branch and opens a pull request against the main branch. The PR description summarizes the changes, links to any related issues, and lists reviewers. Automated CI checks run linting, link validation, and spell-check.3. <!-- block: work --> Peer Review  ```yaml  step_type: "decision"  next: "Technical Review"  condition: "all reviewer comments resolved"  input: "Pull Request"  output: "Review Checklist"  output_status: "reviewed"  tool: "GitHub"  ```Assigned reviewers examine the PR for content quality, clarity, structure, and style compliance. They leave inline comments and request changes when needed. The author addresses feedback with additional commits. Review is complete when all threads are resolved and at least one reviewer approves.4. <!-- block: work --> Technical Review  ```yaml  step_type: "decision"  next: "Approval"  condition: "technical accuracy confirmed"  input: "Pull Request"  output: "Review Checklist"  output_status: "verified"  tool: "GitHub"  ```A subject-matter expert reviews the document for technical accuracy. This gate ensures that specifications, numbers, code snippets, diagrams, and references are correct. The technical reviewer can request changes or clarifications before signing off.5. <!-- block: work --> Approval  ```yaml  step_type: "decision"  next: "Merge and Publish"  condition: "peer and technical reviews passed"  input: "Pull Request"  output: "Review Checklist"  output_status: "approved"  tool: "GitHub"  ```The approver reviews the full PR GÇö content, technical review results, and CI status GÇö and makes the final decision. If any review gate is incomplete or CI checks fail, the PR is sent back. Once approved, the PR is ready for merge.6. <!-- block: work --> Merge and Publish  ```yaml  step_type: "task"  input: "Pull Request"  output: "Published Document"  output_status: "final"  tool: "GitHub"  ```The approved PR is merged into the main branch. A CI/CD pipeline automatically deploys the updated documentation to the knowledge base site. The Knowledge Manager is notified of the publication. The document is now live and available to the organization.# <!-- block: concepts --> artifact* <!-- block: artifact --> Draft Document  The raw Markdown file created by the author during the drafting phase. Contains the full content but has not yet passed any review gate.* <!-- block: artifact --> Pull Request  The GitHub pull request that encapsulates all changes, discussion threads, CI results, and review decisions. Serves as the audit trail for the entire workflow.* <!-- block: artifact --> Review Checklist  A summary of review outcomes for each gate: peer review comments, technical review findings, and approval decision. Attached to the PR as a comment or checklist.* <!-- block: artifact --> Style Guide  The organization's authoritative guide for Markdown formatting, frontmatter conventions, heading hierarchy, image handling, and tone of voice. Referenced by authors and reviewers during drafting and review.* <!-- block: artifact --> Published Document  The final version of the document deployed to the knowledge base site. Immutable once published; subsequent changes go through the same workflow as a new version.* <!-- block: artifact --> Changelog Entry  A record of what changed in this publication cycle. Generated from commit messages and PR descriptions. Maintained in a central changelog file for traceability.# <!-- block: concepts --> tools* <!-- block: tools --> Markdown Editor  Any Markdown-compatible editor used by authors during drafting. Examples include VS Code with Markdown extensions, Obsidian, or Typora. Must support frontmatter YAML and GFM syntax.* <!-- block: tools --> GitHub  The platform hosting the Git repository. Manages branches, pull requests, code reviews, CI/CD integration, and access control. Central hub for the entire workflow.* <!-- block: tools --> CI/CD Pipeline  Automated checks triggered on each PR: markdownlint for style compliance, link checker for broken URLs, spell-check, and build preview. Must pass before merge.* <!-- block: tools --> Knowledge Base Platform  The publishing target GÇö a static site generator like Docsify, MkDocs, or Docusaurus that renders Markdown files into a browsable knowledge base. Deployed automatically after merge.# <!-- block: concepts --> roles* <!-- block: roles --> Author  ```yaml  scope: internal  ```  The person who writes the document. Responsible for content accuracy, formatting, and following the style guide. Initiates the workflow by creating a draft and submitting a pull request.* <!-- block: roles --> Reviewer  ```yaml  scope: internal  ```  A peer who reviews the document for clarity, completeness, structure, and adherence to the style guide. Provides feedback via PR comments and requests changes when necessary.* <!-- block: roles --> Technical Reviewer  ```yaml  scope: internal  ```  A subject-matter expert who validates technical accuracy. Ensures that facts, code samples, diagrams, and specifications are correct before approval.* <!-- block: roles --> Approver  ```yaml  scope: internal  ```  The final decision-maker who authorizes publication. Responsible for ensuring all review cycles are complete and the document meets organizational standards.* <!-- block: roles --> Knowledge Manager  ```yaml  scope: internal  ```  The owner of the knowledge base. Maintains the information architecture, taxonomy, style guide, and publishing pipeline. Onboards new authors and reviews analytics.* <!-- block: roles --> External Contributor  ```yaml  scope: external  ```  A guest contributor from outside the organization who submits documentation changes. Has limited write access and requires additional review gates.# <!-- block: concepts --> position* <!-- block: position --> Technical Writer  A position that assumes the Author and Reviewer roles. Responsible for producing and reviewing documentation as part of the regular workflow.* <!-- block: position --> Senior Developer  A position that assumes the Technical Reviewer and Reviewer roles. Provides technical accuracy validation and peer review for documentation related to their domain.* <!-- block: position --> Team Lead  A position that assumes the Approver and Reviewer roles. Makes final approval decisions for their team's documentation and participates in peer reviews.* <!-- block: position --> Documentation Lead  A position that assumes the Knowledge Manager and Approver roles. Owns the knowledge base, defines standards, and oversees the publication pipeline.* <!-- block: position --> DevOps Engineer  A position that assumes the Technical Reviewer role for infrastructure and deployment documentation. Maintains the CI/CD pipeline and knowledge base platform.# <!-- block: concepts --> person* <!-- block: person --> Alex Chen  A Technical Writer who drafts and reviews documentation for the platform team. Experienced in Markdown and technical communication.* <!-- block: person --> Maria Santos  A Senior Developer on the backend team who reviews API and architecture documentation. Deep technical knowledge of the core systems.* <!-- block: person --> James Wilson  A Team Lead for the frontend team. Approves documentation for his team and reviews cross-team contributions.* <!-- block: person --> Priya Patel  The Documentation Lead. Manages the knowledge base, defines the style guide, and trains new authors.* <!-- block: person --> Tom+ís Rivera  A DevOps Engineer who maintains the CI/CD pipeline and reviews infrastructure documentation.* <!-- block: person --> Sam Taylor  An External Contributor from a partner organization who submits integration documentation. Requires additional review gates.# <!-- block: matrices --> item-markers matrix| Item \ Marker | complexity || :--- | :---: || Draft Document | 1 || Submit Pull Request | 1 || Peer Review | 2 || Technical Review | 3 || Approval | 2 || Merge and Publish | 1 || Pull Request | 1 || Review Checklist | 1 || Style Guide | 2 || Published Document | 1 || Changelog Entry | 1 || Markdown Editor | 1 || GitHub | 1 || CI/CD Pipeline | 3 || Knowledge Base Platform | 2 || Author | -
